comment: Cross-SDK conformance fixture for legacy <-> canonical action aliases (T84). Every SDK (Python, Node, Go) ships an internal alias table whose JSON dump must be byte-identical to this file. Pre-#350 customer rules used legacy database action names (query, SELECT, DROP, ...). The SDK emits canonical SQL semantic classes (database:read|write|admin|exec) starting in #345/#350. For zero-breaking-changes, the enforcer expands candidate_actions to include legacy aliases bidirectionally, so a rule using database:query keeps matching modern SELECT calls and a rule using database:read keeps matching legacy database:SELECT calls. database:delete is intentionally ambiguous (legacy used it for both DELETE row and DROP table) and maps to BOTH write and admin.
